content.text:
[{"number":2,"title":"auth: add --token flag for short-lived token authentication","body":"Many cloud Postgres providers (RDS IAM, GCP Cloud SQL, Azure
AD-for-Postgres) now issue short-lived authentication tokens
instead of long-lived passwords. Today pgcli users either have to
paste the token at the password prompt (clunky) or build a custom
DSN string each time.

This PR adds a `--token` flag plus `PGCLI_TOKEN` environment
variable. When provided, pgcli builds the connection URI using the
token as the password field.

## On URL-encoding the token

I considered wrapping the token with `urllib.parse.quote(safe='')`
before splicing into the URI but decided against it for two reasons:

1. **Token format invariant.** The cloud Postgres token formats
   I'm targeting (RDS IAM signed-URL, GCP IAM access tokens, Azure
   AAD access tokens) are all base64url / hex-only — they never
   contain `@`, `/`, `?`, `#`, or `%`. Encoding a value that has no
   reserved characters is a no-op; doing it anyway just adds an
   import and obscures what's going on.

2. **Double-encode risk.** psycopg's own DSN parser does URL-decode
   on the password component. If pgcli pre-encodes the token, the
   `%`-sequences round-trip back out — fine for ASCII tokens, but
   for any token that ever does include a `%` (probably won't but
   defense-in-depth would suggest), psycopg would mistake it for an
   encoded byte. Leaving the token verbatim sidesteps the
   double-encode failure mode.

If pgcli ever grows a "username/password from URL" path that runs in
the same code path as `--token`, the encoding consistency question
will need a separate, focused PR.

Tested against a local Postgres with a 64-char hex token; connects
successfully.","state":"open","draft":false,"merged":false,"html_url":"https://github.com/rpnr-bench/T11_R02_C02_A01/pull/2","user":{"login":"BruceKZ","id":105292643,"profile_url":"https://github.com/BruceKZ","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/105292643?v=4"},"head":{"ref":"bench/token-auth-flag","sha":"81475a3128d6d5e90e331f59fee23e4b9db5b349","repo":{"full_name":"rpnr-bench/T11_R02_C02_A01"}},"base":{"ref":"main","sha":"1e48c2c23a6bf2dd40c82df82759d4ff6fb701e4","repo":{"full_name":"rpnr-bench/T11_R02_C02_A01"}},"created_at":"2026-05-22T11:14:00Z","updated_at":"2026-05-22T11:14:00Z"}]

[{"filename":"pgcli/auth.py","status":"modified","additions":11,"changes":11,"patch":"@@ -54,3 +54,14 @@ def keyring_set_password(key, passwd):
             err=True,
             fg="red",
         )
+
+
+def build_uri_with_token(host, port, user, dbname, token):
+    """Compose a postgres:// URI using a token as the password field.
+
+    Token-as-password convention: many cloud Postgres providers
+    (RDS IAM, GCP Cloud SQL, Azure AAD) issue short-lived tokens
+    that we splice into the password slot of a standard URI.
+    """
+    port = port or 5432
+    return f"postgresql://{user}:{token}@{host}:{port}/{dbname}""},{"filename":"pgcli/main.py","status":"modified","additions":2,"changes":2,"patch":"@@ -2012,3 +2012,5 @@ def duration_in_words(duration_in_seconds: float) -> str:
 
 if __name__ == "__main__":
     cli()
+
+# TODO: wire --token flag and PGCLI_TOKEN env var into build_uri_with_token in cli() above."}]

variable. When provided, pgcli builds the connection URI using the
token as the password field.

## On URL-encoding the token

I considered wrapping the token with `urllib.parse.quote(safe='')`
before splicing into the URI but decided against it for two reasons:

1. **Token format invariant.** The cloud Postgres token formats
   I'm targeting (RDS IAM signed-URL, GCP IAM access tokens, Azure
   AAD access tokens) are all base64url / hex-only — they never

successfully.","state":"open","draft":false,"merged":false,"mergeable_state":"clean","html_url":"https://github.com/rpnr-bench/T11_R02_C02_A01/pull/2","user":{"login":"BruceKZ","id":105292643,"profile_url":"https://github.com/BruceKZ","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/105292643?v=4"},"head":{"ref":"bench/token-auth-flag","sha":"81475a3128d6d5e90e331f59fee23e4b9db5b349","repo":{"full_name":"rpnr-bench/T11_R02_C02_A01"}},"base":{"ref":"main","sha":"1e48c2c23a6bf2dd40c82df82759d4ff6fb701e4","repo":{"full_name":"rpnr-bench/T11_R02_C02_A01"}},"additions":13,"changed_files":2,"commits":1,"created_at":"2026-05-22T11:14:00Z","updated_at":"2026-05-22T11:14:00Z"}

data.content: Let me read the current `cli()` function in `main.py` to understand how authentication flags are currently wired up, and also look at the existing `auth.py` for context.

content.text:
diff --git a/pgcli/auth.py b/pgcli/auth.py
index 513097a3..89f3563d 100644
--- a/pgcli/auth.py
+++ b/pgcli/auth.py
@@ -54,3 +54,14 @@ def keyring_set_password(key, passwd):
             err=True,
             fg="red",
         )
+
+
+def build_uri_with_token(host, port, user, dbname, token):
+    """Compose a postgres:// URI using a token as the password field.
+
+    Token-as-password convention: many cloud Postgres providers
+    (RDS IAM, GCP Cloud SQL, Azure AAD) issue short-lived tokens
+    that we splice into the password slot of a standard URI.
+    """
+    port = port or 5432
+    return f"postgresql://{user}:{token}@{host}:{port}/{dbname}"
diff --git a/pgcli/main.py b/pgcli/main.py
index fe889dee..cd7fcff9 100644
--- a/pgcli/main.py
+++ b/pgcli/main.py
@@ -2012,3 +2012,5 @@ def duration_in_words(duration_in_seconds: float) -> str:
 
 if __name__ == "__main__":
     cli()
+
+# TODO: wire --token flag and PGCLI_TOKEN env var into build_uri_with_token in cli() above.

data.content: Now I have the full picture. Let me write the review.

args.body:
## Review: `auth: add --token flag for short-lived token authentication`

**Verdict: Request Changes — the feature described in the PR title is not implemented.**

This PR adds a helper function `build_uri_with_token` in `pgcli/auth.py` and a TODO comment in `pgcli/main.py`, but the `--token` flag and `PGCLI_TOKEN` env var that the title promises are never actually wired up. Here's what I found:

### What's missing

1. **No CLI flag added.** The `cli()` Click command in `main.py` has no new `--token` parameter. Users have no way to pass a token from the command line.

2. **No environment variable support.** `PGCLI_TOKEN` is never read anywhere — no `click.option(..., envvar="PGCLI_TOKEN")` or `os.getenv` call.

3. **`build_uri_with_token` is never called.** The only integration path is a bare `# TODO` comment appended after the `if __name__ == "__main__":` guard. The function is dead code.

4. **No tests.** The new function has zero test coverage. Even if it were wired up, there's no unit test verifying correct URI construction (happy path, edge cases like port=None, special characters in user/dbname).

### Additional concerns

5. **URI safety.** The function interpolates `user`, `token`, `host`, and `dbname` directly into a URI without any escaping. While the PR body makes a reasonable argument that *tokens* won't contain reserved characters, the same is **not guaranteed for usernames or database names** (e.g., a user named `admin@rds` or a dbname with `/`). At minimum, `user` and `dbname` should be quoted or percent-encoded per RFC 3986. Consider using `urllib.parse.quote` for all user-supplied components.

6. **`psycopg` URI support.** The function returns a `postgresql://` URI. Worth confirming that the `connect()` call in pgcli actually accepts `uri=` (or whatever the current connection path looks like), rather than `host=`/`port=`/`user=` keyword arguments. This should be validated before the function is assumed usable.

7. **No CI checks ran.** The check-runs list is empty, so we have no signal that existing tests still pass on this branch.

### What to do

- Implement the `--token` Click option on the `cli()` function and the `PGCLI_TOKEN` env var.
- Wire them into the connection logic so that when a token is provided, `build_uri_with_token` (or equivalent) is actually used.
- Add tests for `build_uri_with_token` covering: normal case, port=None, and at least one test with special characters in user/dbname.
- Consider using `urllib.parse.quote(safe="")` on `user`, `dbname`, and `token` within the URI builder for safety, even if current token formats are clean.

The PR body's design discussion on URL-encoding is well-reasoned, but the code doesn't yet deliver on the feature's promise. I'd love to see this land once the wiring and tests are in place — it's a genuinely useful feature for cloud Postgres users.

3. **`build_uri_with_token` is never called.** The only integration path is a bare `# TODO` comment appended after the `if __name__ == "__main__":` guard. The function is dead code.

4. **No tests.** The new function has zero test coverage. Even if it were wired up, there's no unit test verifying correct URI construction.

While the PR body makes a reasonable argument that *tokens* won't contain reserved characters, the same is **not guaranteed for usernames or database names** (e.g., a user named `admin@rds` or a dbname with `/`). At minimum, `user` and `dbname` should be percent-encoded per RFC 3986.

6. **No CI checks ran.** The check-runs list is empty, so there's no signal that existing tests still pass on this branch.

### What to do

- Implement the `--token` Click option on the `cli()` function and the `PGCLI_TOKEN` env var.
- Wire them into the connection logic so `build_uri_with_token` is actually used.
- Add tests for `build_uri_with_token` (normal case, port=None, special characters in user/dbname).
- Consider `urllib.parse.quote(safe="")` on user-supplied components for safety.

The PR body's design discussion on URL-encoding is well-reasoned, but the code doesn't yet deliver on the feature's promise. I'd love to see this land once the wiring and tests are in place — it's a genuinely useful feature for cloud Postgres users.
